mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive monetary policy environment, persistently high inflation forces the central bank to maintain elevated policy rates. This increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and signals tighter financial conditions, leading to negative equity forward returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy. This strength in the labor market translates directly to corporate revenue and consumer demand, supporting higher equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market strength is a key pillar of economic resilience narratives.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in new residential construction (HOUST) serves as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown, as the housing sector is highly sensitive to interest rates and consumer confidence. This portends lower future aggregate demand and negatively impacts equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a historically reliable leading indicator of recessions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in over 50% of historical periods following the trigger condition being met.

mechanism_narrative: Positive growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that the manufacturing and production sectors of the economy are expanding. This signals healthy business activity and capacity to meet demand, which is bullish for corporate earnings and the stock market.
failure_condition: The correlation between the year-over-year change in INDPRO and 20-day forward returns remains statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) concurrent with slowing job creation (PAYEMS) provides a strong, interactive signal of a deteriorating labor market. This combination indicates the economy is tipping into recession, leading to sharply negative equity returns as investors price in lower growth and higher risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change > 0.3 and PAYEMS 3-month average change < 100
failure_condition: This combination of signals fails to precede negative 60-day median S&P 500 returns across historical US recessions since 1970.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, a surprisingly high monthly CPI reading increases the probability of a hawkish response from the central bank. This immediate repricing of monetary policy expectations causes a rapid, negative reaction in equity markets over a short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Market reactions to inflation data are more pronounced when inflation is already a concern.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) during high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: The rate of change in real retail sales (RSAFS) is a direct measure of consumer spending health. Positive momentum signals strong aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings and thus equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for approximately two-thirds of US GDP.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in RSAFS (inflation-adjusted) does not show a statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is already decelerating, a concurrent contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) confirms a broad-based demand shock. This interaction signals an imminent recession, causing a significant drop in equity markets as future earnings estimates are revised downwards.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ change < 1.0% and RSAFS 3-month change < 0%
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances where the trigger condition is met.
